title: ADR 0007: Claim takes task-only, task-CAS first, release un-claims
description: Phase 2 moved tasks into NATS KV (ADR 0005). That created three second-order decisions the existing docs don't capture: (1) Claim's file list belongs to the task record, not the caller — so the signature drops the files arg and becomes Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl); (2) the acquisition order flips to task-CAS first, then holds, so loser sees ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed semantically rather than ErrHeldByAnother as a substrate side-effect; (3) the release closure must CAS-undo the task claim in addition to releasing holds, otherwise a crash between release and CloseTask leaks the claim forever (tasks don't have TTL). This ADR records the three choices together since they are entailed by one another.
notes: ADR 0007 lands three coupled decisions: (1) Claim signature drops files (task record carries them per ADR 0005); (2) task-CAS first, then holds — loser sees ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed semantically; (3) release un-claims the task record in addition to releasing holds, preventing permanent leaks on crash between release and CloseTask. Amends ADR 0002 with a supersedes-in-part note. Invariant 16 (release undoes full acquisition) is added by issue D.

title: coord.CloseTask(ctx, taskID, reason) — closer-identity invariant
description: Replace the ErrNotImplemented stub. CAS-update the task entry to status=closed, set closed_reason, closed_by=AgentID, closed_at=now. Enforces invariant 12: closer must equal current claimed_by. If the caller's AgentID does not match, return ErrAgentMismatch without modifying the entry. If the task is already closed, return ErrTaskNotOpen (add to errors.go). Asserts invariants 1, 2, 8. No admin override in Phase 2 — a future ADR can add one if operators need to forcibly close a stuck task.

title: coord.Ready(ctx) ([]Task, error) — KV prefix scan
description: Replace the ErrNotImplemented stub. Scan the tasks bucket, filter to entries with status=open AND claimed_by empty, decode them into Task values, sort by created_at ascending, and return. Bound the returned slice length (via coord.Config.MaxReadyReturn or similar — pick a default and document it). Asserts invariants 1 and 8. Each returned Task must carry enough populated fields for the caller to decide what to claim; update coord.Task to expose the necessary accessors if it doesn't already.

title: Extend coord.Claim to update task entry atomically
description: Extend coord.Claim to own the full task acquisition (not just holds). Per ADR 0007 the signature changes to Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl) — drop the files arg. Implementation order: (1) read task record from NATS KV; assert status=open and claimed_by empty; (2) CAS-update the task record to claimed_by=agentID, status=claimed; if CAS loses, return ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed immediately (no wasted hold-acquire); (3) acquire holds on the task's declared files; if any hold fails, CAS-undo the task claim and return the underlying error. The returned release closure (idempotent per invariant 7) must CAS-undo the task claim AND release all holds — release is symmetrical with Claim's work. ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed is the new coord sentinel (add to coord/errors.go as part of this issue).

title: coord.OpenTask(ctx, title, files) (TaskID, error)
description: New public method on coord that opens a task entry. Generates a TaskID per ADR 0005, writes an initial task record (status=open, claimed_by empty, files sorted/deduplicated) via CAS-Create on the tasks bucket. Asserts invariants 1 (ctx non-nil), 2 (TaskID shape), 4 (files sorted/bounded/absolute), and 11 (claimed_by empty iff status!=claimed). Returns the TaskID on success. ErrAlreadyExists is a programmer error at this level — generated IDs should not collide; if a CAS-Create loses the generator is buggy and we panic via assert rather than silently retrying.

title: Invariants 11-16 for task state
description: Append to docs/invariants.md: (11) claimed_by non-empty iff status=claimed; (12) closer must equal current claimed_by, no admin override in Phase 2; (13) status transitions are a fixed DAG — open→claimed→closed and open→closed; no backwards; (14) task value size ≤ Config.MaxTaskValueSize (8 KB per ADR 0005); (15) TaskID matches the shape defined in ADR 0005 (`<proj>-<8char lowercase alnum>`); (16) release closure undoes the full Claim acquisition, CAS-un-claiming the task record in addition to releasing holds (per ADR 0007). This issue also adds Config.MaxTaskValueSize as a new bounded-limit field, rejected-on-empty by Config.Validate.

title: Implement internal/tasks/ package on NATS KV
description: Mirror the internal/holds/ layout. Expose a Manager type with Open/Close, plus Create/Update/Get/List/Watch primitives over a NATS JetStream KV bucket. CAS-atomic via revision-gated jetstream.KeyValue.Create and .Update (same pattern as internal/holds after dt7). JSON Task record shape per ADR 0005. Substrate-hidden per ADR 0003 — no NATS types leak out of the package. This is the data-plane package the coord methods in later issues build on.

title: ADR 0006: Scope-narrow ADR 0004 to code artifacts only
description: ADR 0004 was written assuming tasks lived in fossil and adopted 'fossil fork + chat notify on conflict' as the resolution model. With tasks now in NATS KV (ADR 0005), task-state conflicts resolve via CAS-lose returning ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed — no merge, no fork, no chat notify on that path. This ADR narrows 0004's scope to code artifacts only, and adds a superseding note at the top of 0004.md pointing here. Fossil's fork-and-notify model still applies to code when fossil enters in Phase 5+; it just no longer describes task state.

title: ADR 0005: Tasks in NATS JetStream KV
description: Decision record for the task-substrate choice. Covers bucket naming, key shape, JSON value schema, status enum, ID shape, KV history depth, MaxValueSize, retention-on-close posture. Mirrors ADRs 0001-0004 (Nygard Status/Context/Decision/Consequences format). Explicitly supersedes the README's 'tasks-as-files-in-fossil' plan. This is the architectural anchor for the rest of Phase 2 — every other Phase 2 issue should be able to cite a concrete decision from this ADR.
notes: Decisions: bucket=agent-infra-tasks (parallel to agent-infra-holds), key=<taskID> flat, value=JSON with id/title/status/claimed_by/files/parent/context/created_at/updated_at/closed_at/closed_by/closed_reason/schema_version, status enum=open|claimed|closed only, ID shape=<proj>-<8char lowercase alnum nanoid> (~41 bits entropy, URL-safe, chat-friendly; UUIDv7 rejected — Ready sort is client-side), history=8 (Config.TaskHistoryDepth), MaxValueSize=8 KB, no MaxAge (closed tasks retained; Phase 4 compaction will prune). Explicitly supersedes README tasks-as-files-in-fossil plan. ADR 0004 narrowed to code artifacts by ADR 0006.

title: Make holds.Announce CAS-atomic (replace read-then-write)
description:
Phase 1 concurrent-claim proof (owp) passes because NATS JetStream serializes Put operations enough that racing Announces never observe a torn read-then-write. The current implementation in internal/holds/holds.go is not CAS-protected: a reader could decode a hold, decide it is vacant (expired or empty), and Put its own entry without a revision-gated compare. Two processes racing the same file with identically-timed Announces could, in principle, both believe they own it.

Phase 5 chaos tests (under DST-style scheduling) are where this is likely to surface. The fix is to use nats.KeyValue.Update(key, value, revision) — the JetStream KV CAS primitive — keyed on the revision observed during the read. On Update returning ErrKeyExists or a revision mismatch, retry the read-decide-update loop.

ADR 0004 (fossil fork on conflict) absorbs residual inconsistency downstream, but the hold primitive should still be the tight one — conflicts should be rare, not 'occasionally the read-then-write torn, and we forked'.
notes:
CAS-atomic Announce shipped.

Design: Announce now runs a bounded retry loop (maxCASRetries=8). Each iteration:
Get the current entry, decide (vacant → Create; same-agent → Update renew;
expired other-agent → Update take-over; live other-agent → ErrHeldByAnother),
then write with the observed revision. Revision mismatch loops; all other
errors are terminal. Extracted helpers announceAttempt, decideAnnounce,
casVacant, casRevision, stamped to stay well under the funlen=70 cap.

Error sentinels (nats.go v1.49.0):
- jetstream.ErrKeyExists (Create on existing key)
- jetstream.JSErrCodeStreamWrongLastSequence = 10071 (Update revision mismatch;
  also the underlying code for ErrKeyExists).
Verified by reading the vendored source at kv.go:1052-1116 and errors.go:320-322.
isCASConflict checks errors.Is(err, ErrKeyExists) AND the raw APIError code so
a library regrouping won't silently convert conflicts to 'unknown error'.

Retry bound: 8. Rationale: each retry is one KV round-trip; pathological
contention converges in 2-3 rounds; 8 leaves slack without letting a pinned
Announce stall indefinitely. Documented in the const godoc.

Test coverage (internal/holds/holds_cas_test.go):
- TestAnnounce_CAS_RetryOnConcurrentWrite: hook + raw KV Put wedge to exercise
  retry path (accepts either scheduling — DST will flush bias in Phase 5).
- TestAnnounce_CAS_ConcurrentContention: 16 goroutines race, assert exactly one
  winner, 15 ErrHeldByAnother, 0 other errors, bucket reflects winner.
- TestAnnounce_CAS_RenewUnderContention: 8 same-agent renewals race, all
  succeed.

Test seams added (non-invasive): KVForTest, SetCASRetryHookForTest,
EncodeForTest, KeyForTest in internal/holds/testhooks.go — all ForTest suffix.

Public API: unchanged. Manager / Announce / Release / WhoHas / Subscribe
signatures intact.

Verification: make check OK; go test -race -count=10 ./internal/holds/...
./coord/... runs in ~13s, no flakiness over multiple runs.

title: CI + lint scaffolding (gofmt, vet, staticcheck, errcheck, race, bounds)
description: TigerStyle discipline from day one requires CI on every push and PR: gofmt check, go vet, staticcheck, errcheck, go test -race, line-length <= 100 cols, function-length <= 70 lines, no TODOs in non-test code. Add .github/workflows/ci.yml; add a Makefile target (make check) mirroring CI so contributors run it locally; add a pre-commit hook that catches the fast checks before push. Blocks the API freeze (3ps) so the first code lands under CI, not before.
design: Use golangci-lint with a repo-local config (.golangci.yml) to get staticcheck, errcheck, gofmt, line-length, and function-length in one invocation. Race detector runs via go test -race in a separate CI step (slower). TODO check is a grep that fails on TODO outside _test.go files — every TODO becomes a beads issue instead.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow green on main; make check runs the full suite locally; pre-commit hook catches gofmt and vet issues; a deliberate PR with a too-long function and a TODO outside tests fails CI

title: Record docs/invariants.md + internal/assert helpers (TigerStyle)
description: The 2026-04-18 TigerStyle review produced a canonical list of runtime invariants for the coord surface. Record them as docs/invariants.md (canonical prose) and implement a small internal/assert package: Precondition(cond bool, msg string, args ...any), Postcondition, NoError, NotNil, NotEmpty. Every public coord method calls Precondition for arg validation at entry. Keeps TigerStyle 'assertions as documentation' posture and avoids scattering ad-hoc nil-checks throughout the code. Blocks the API freeze (3ps) so signatures incorporate the invariants.
design: internal/assert is NOT exported. Assertions trigger panics (programmer errors — bugs, not recoverable). Config.Validate() is the one exception: it returns an error because it runs at Open with user-provided data (operator error). Invariants doc covers: ctx non-nil, IDs non-empty, files slice bounded and sorted, TTL bounded, Claim atomicity, Release idempotency, Coord lifecycle (no use before Open / after Close), no naked returns.
acceptance_criteria: docs/invariants.md lists the 10 invariants with rationale; internal/assert package has 5 helpers with unit tests including positive and negative cases; godoc on each coord public method references the invariants it enforces

title: Wire coord.Claim to internal/holds (scoped-hold implementation)
description: Implement coord.Claim(ctx, taskID, files) by (a) announcing holds on all requested files via internal/holds, (b) reading or updating the task representation to mark it claimed, (c) returning (Task, release func, error). The release closure tears down all holds atomically. If (a) fails after partial acquisition, roll back already-announced holds before returning error. Validates the define-away-error-classes commitment.
design: Holds acquired in deterministic order (sorted file paths) to prevent cross-claim deadlocks. Release is safe to call multiple times (idempotent). Partial-acquisition failure: rollback, then return an error naming the files we could not hold.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: two goroutines race Claim on overlapping files — one succeeds, the other fails with a clear held-by-<agent> error; both release cleanly; no orphaned NATS KV entries
notes: Shipped: coord.Claim wired to internal/holds. Added NATSURL to coord.Config (rejected empty in Validate). coord.Open dials NATS, opens holds.Manager on bucket 'agent-infra-holds'; rollback on partial failure. coord.Close tears down Manager then NATS; idempotent. Claim announces holds in sorted order; rollback releases best-effort on partial failure and maps holds.ErrHeldByAnother to coord.ErrHeldByAnother. Release closure uses sync.Once (invariant 7) and silently swallows holds.ErrClosed so defer release() is safe after Coord.Close. 5 new tests (happy path, contention/atomicity, release-then-reclaim, idempotent release, release-after-close). Function bodies within 70-line cap (Claim=25, assertClaimPreconditions=33); longest line 74 chars. make check green under -race -count=2.

title: Implement internal/holds on NATS KV with TTL
description: Internal (unexported) package. NATS KV bucket named holds.current keyed by file path, value = {agent_id, claimed_at, checkout_path, ttl}. Primitives: Announce(file, agent, ttl), Release(file, agent), WhoHas(file), Subscribe(pattern). Consumed only by coord — no external API. Unit tests via the embedded NATS harness.
design: TTL handles crash recovery (abandoned holds auto-expire). Announce is idempotent for same agent. Release is a no-op when hold is missing or belongs to a different agent — defines away an error class callers would otherwise have to handle.
acceptance_criteria: internal/holds has 4 primitives; unit tests cover happy-path announce+release, TTL expiry, WhoHas positive+negative, Subscribe receives announce events; all green on the embedded harness
notes: Implemented internal/holds on NATS JetStream KV. 4 primitives (Announce/Release/WhoHas/Subscribe), lazy TTL expiry, URL-escape key encoding (/→/, %→=), bucket-wide MaxAge as substrate backstop. 22 tests pass under go test -race -count=2.

title: Embedded NATS test harness (reusable fixture)
description: Reusable Go test fixture that spins up an in-process nats-server for unit and integration tests. Port the pattern from reference/EdgeSync/leaf/agent/nats_mesh.go. Exposes natstest.NewTestServer(t) returning (*nats.Conn, cleanup) using t.Cleanup. All subsequent hold and coord tests consume this.
acceptance_criteria: natstest package exists at internal/testutil/natstest; one example test uses it; harness tears down cleanly with no leaked goroutines

title: Freeze coord public API surface (5 methods, opaque types, stubs only)
description: Define coord/coord.go and coord/types.go with the 5 methods (Claim, Close, Ready, Post, Ask) and opaque types (Task, TaskID, Hold). All method bodies return ErrNotImplemented. This pins the public surface so internals can evolve without API churn and gives downstream consumers something to integrate against from day one. No NATS subject strings, no fossil txn refs in any public signature.
design: coord is the sole exported package. Types: Task (opaque struct), TaskID (string newtype), Hold (opaque handle). Signatures: Claim(ctx, TaskID, files []string) (Task, func(), error) — scoped hold via return-release closure. Ready(ctx) ([]Task, error). Close(ctx, TaskID, reason string) error. Post(ctx, thread string, msg []byte) error. Ask(ctx, recipient, question string) (string, error).
acceptance_criteria: coord/ compiles; all 5 methods godoc-documented; no internal/* types leak through public signatures; tests verify stubs return ErrNotImplemented

title: Bootstrap Go module, workspace, and import-boundary lint
description: Phase 1 cannot proceed without a compilable Go module. Create go.mod (module github.com/danmestas/agent-infra), go.work.example pointing at canonical sibling paths (../EdgeSync, ../go-libfossil) per README Development setup, and a pre-commit hook that fails if the repo imports from EdgeSync/internal/ or go-libfossil/internal/. This is the keep-the-boundary-honest guardrail from README.
acceptance_criteria: go build ./... succeeds; go.work.example paths match README Development setup; pre-commit hook blocks an import of EdgeSync/internal/anything when verified in test

title: Record Phase 1 ADRs: public surface, scoped holds, substrate hiding, conflict model
description: Four architectural commitments from the 2026-04-18 Ousterhout review need persistent recording as ADRs under docs/adr/. (1) coord is the sole exported package (closes README Open Q #3). (2) Scoped holds via closure/return-release, not announce/release pairs. (3) Substrate details (NATS subjects, fossil txn refs) never appear in public signatures. (4) Conflict resolution is fossil-fork + chat-notify (closes README Open Q #4). Also update README Open Questions section to mark #3 and #4 resolved with pointers to the ADRs.
acceptance_criteria: docs/adr/0001-public-surface.md, 0002-scoped-holds.md, 0003-substrate-hiding.md, 0004-conflict-resolution.md exist; README Open Questions #3 and #4 resolved with ADR references

title: Draft reference/CAPABILITIES.md
description: Draft reference/CAPABILITIES.md as a side-by-side table: beads feature | our planned equivalent | gap. Input: the two Phase 0 audit summaries. Output: a single sanctioned markdown doc at reference/CAPABILITIES.md. Phase 0 task from GETTING_STARTED.md.
close_reason: Drafted reference/CAPABILITIES.md — 13 sections covering task lifecycle, DAG, persistence, agent workflow, messaging, scheduling, compaction, templates, integrity, CLI/plugin, non-goals, our additions, and parked questions. Phase 0 audit sequence complete.

title: Read beads internal/ packages and data model
description: Study the Dolt-backed data model inside beads. Read reference/beads/internal/ packages to understand how tasks, dependencies, and metadata are modeled, persisted, and merged. Produce a <=400-word summary. Phase 0 audit task from GETTING_STARTED.md.

title: Read beads agent config (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, claude-plugin)
description: Study how beads presents itself to coding agents. Read reference/beads/AGENTS.md, AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md, CLAUDE.md, and the claude-plugin/ directory. Produce a <=400-word summary: what bd commands agents are directed to use, what conventions beads asks agents to follow, how it structures its claude-plugin integration. Phase 0 audit task from GETTING_STARTED.md.

title: Smoke test: two-agent concurrent Claim on same task
description: End-to-end test: two Coords (different AgentIDs) share a NATS server. Both call Claim on the same taskID. Exactly one succeeds; the other gets ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed (NOT ErrHeldByAnother — task CAS fires first per ADR 0007). Winner's release closure un-claims the task AND releases holds; verify by calling Claim from a third Coord on the same taskID after release — must succeed. 25x -race clean, runtime under 2s.

title: Smoke test: single-agent lifecycle (OpenTask→Claim→CloseTask)
description: End-to-end test in coord/task_smoke_test.go. One Coord, one embedded NATS server (reuse the Phase 1 test harness). Sequence: OpenTask(title, [fileA, fileB]) returns a TaskID; Ready() returns the one task with status=open; Claim(taskID, files, ttl) succeeds and returns a release closure; Ready() now returns empty (task is claimed); CloseTask(taskID, 'done') succeeds; Ready() remains empty. Assert each intermediate state. Mirrors the shape of claim_smoke_test.go.

title: Smoke test: two concurrent agents claim overlapping files end-to-end
description: End-to-end test (likely coord/claim_smoke_test.go or examples/two-agents/) where two goroutines simulate agents calling coord.Claim on overlapping file sets. Verifies: first claim wins, second fails with a clear error, first releases, second can then claim. Baseline for Phase 5 chaos tests and first proof the scoped-hold ergonomics survive contention.
acceptance_criteria: Test runs under 2s; covers simultaneous-claim contention and claim-release-reclaim sequences
notes: Added coord/claim_smoke_test.go (214 LoC, 2 tests). TestClaimSmoke_ConcurrentContention races two agents on overlapping files through the full Open/Claim/Release/Close path against a shared embedded JetStream server, asserts exactly-one-winner without bias, verifies winner release frees the files for loser reclaim, and confirms no orphaned KV entries survive Close. TestClaimSmoke_ReclaimAcrossAgents documents the sequential reclaim path on the two-Coord fixture. Verification: go test -race -count=5 ./coord/... in 4.56s; 25-run stress at 3.3s (130ms/run); make check green.

value: Phase 1 invariants (TigerStyle, 2026-04-18) — asserted in every coord public method: (1) ctx != nil; (2) TaskID non-empty and matches ID shape; (3) agent_id non-empty; (4) files non-empty, len <= MaxHoldsPerClaim, paths absolute, sorted before use; (5) HoldTTL > 0 and <= MaxHoldTTL; (6) Claim is atomic — partial hold acquisition is rolled back before error return; (7) Release closure is idempotent; (8) Coord cannot be used before Open or after Close; (9) Config.Validate runs at Open, panics on invalid; (10) all public methods return (_, error), no swallowed errors. Panics reserved for programmer errors; sentinel errors (ErrHeldByAnother, ErrClaimTimeout, ErrTaskNotFound, ErrAgentMismatch) for operating errors.

ADR 0007 (2026-04-19) flipped coord.Claim: signature is now Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl) — files dropped, read from task record. Order is task-CAS first then holds. Release un-claims the task in addition to releasing holds (otherwise tasks leak on crash between release and CloseTask).

Phase 1 architectural direction (Ousterhout review, 2026-04-18): (1) coord is the sole exported package; internal/holds, internal/tasks, internal/chat are unexported. (2) Scoped holds via closure/return-release, not announce/release pairs. (3) NATS subjects and fossil txn refs NEVER appear in coord public signatures. (4) Conflict resolution = fossil fork + chat notify, not configurable. Closes README Open Questions #3 and #4. ADRs 0001-0004 pending (see agent-infra issue for tracking).

Phase 1 bounded limits (TigerStyle, 2026-04-18) — all live in coord.Config, validated at Open, no silent defaults: HoldTTLDefault, HoldTTLMax, MaxHoldsPerClaim, MaxSubscribers, MaxTaskFiles, OperationTimeout, HeartbeatInterval, NATSReconnectWait, NATSMaxReconnects. Zero-deps posture: stdlib + nats.go + go-libfossil + EdgeSync only; any addition requires an ADR. CI enforces: gofmt, go vet, staticcheck, errcheck, go test -race, line-length <= 100, function-length <= 70, no TODOs in non-test code.

Phase 2 moved tasks from fossil-files to NATS JetStream KV (decided 2026-04-19). Fossil is now for code artifacts only; enters in Phase 5+. ADR 0004's task-state language narrowed to code artifacts by ADR 0006. Compaction of closed tasks into repo ADRs is Phase 4 roadmap.
